channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Stone Cold Steve Austin and Liv Morgan Coming to Fortnite, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
WWE Superstars Stone Cold Steve Austin and Liv Morgan are reportedly joining Fortnite, according to a report by HYPEX on social media.

Austin is one of the most legendary wrestlers of all time, while Morgan is one of the top current WWE Superstars. They join the lineup of previous Fortnite x WWE crossover characters, including Cody Rhodes, John Cena, Becky Lynch, Bianca Belair, and The Undertaker.

WWE legend The Rock is also featured in the game, but he appears in Fortnite lore as The Foundation. That character is also set to return to Fortnite.

Game of Thrones Coming to Fortnite

Additionally, Game of Thrones is heading to Fortnite, officially teased by Epic Games with the message “Winter is Coming.”

In 2026, other franchises like The Office, Ed Edd n’ Edd, and Looney Tunes have also made their way into the open-world game.

WWE is currently in its Biggest Season of the Year

WWE is building toward WrestleMania 42, the company’s biggest event of the year. Fortnite crossovers often coincide with major WWE events such as WrestleMania, SummerSlam, or Survivor Series. Steve Austin remains a timeless wrestling icon, known to most fans.

Liv Morgan, on the other hand, is a current star scheduled to have her first singles match at WrestleMania. She will challenge Stephanie Vaquer for the WWE Women’s World Championship, earning this opportunity by winning the 2026 WWE Royal Rumble. Now, they’re entering the Fortnite Battle Royale.

Insider Gaming will keep players updated on the changes coming to Fortnite. For now, check out when the Italian brain-rot characters are heading to Fortnite.

Are you excited for Stone Cold and Liv Morgan in Fortnite? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: CD Projekt Red Plans Mystery Project Launch in Coming Quarters
item.content:encoded:
CD Projekt Red plans to release an unannounced gaming project in the coming quarters.

CD Projekt Red announced its FY 2025 total earnings. In one of the slides discussing the immediate future, they announced plans to publish one of the unannounced gaming projects they have been developing.

There is some social media speculation that it could be a brand new expansion pack for The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt, but that is not confirmed.

CD Projekt Red has confirmed that they will publish an “announced gaming project” in the coming quarters this year. This lines up with rumors that The Witcher 3 is getting a brand new expansion developed by Fool’s Theory in September I really think this is happening y’all!

More on CD Projekt Red in the Future

Furthermore, the studio announced its latest gaming project, codenamed Hadar. More details about that project can be found at this link. In other news related to The Witcher, the fourth game in the series currently has nearly 500 developers working on the title.

Also, the current trilogy has sold more than 80 million units so far. Learn more about that at this link.

Of note, there are 933 total developers working at the studio; only 26 are currently working on Hadar, and fewer than 20 are working on unnamed other projects. The rest are allocated to the previously mentioned Witcher 4, Cyberpunk, Hadar, Sirius, and shared services.

Insider Gaming will continue to report any and all updates on the studio’s projects. For more Witcher content, check out the latest news on the Netflix series, slated to end this year.

What do you think the 2026 CD Projekt Red release will be? Are you hoping for another expansion pack for The Witcher 3? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: Crimson Desert Launch Pulls Huge Twitch and Steam Numbers
item.content:encoded:
Crimson Desert launches with impressive Twitch viewership and Steam player counts, even as review scores remain mixed.

Crimson Desert is the new open-world game from Pearl Abyss, and it launched on March 19. It is already the number one-streamed game on Twitch. The game currently has nearly 500,000 viewers on the major streaming platform.

On SteamDB, the game has a concurrent player count of over 131,000. It’s currently the number six-streamed software on Steam. The title is currently outperforming Marvel Rivals, ARC Raiders, Overwatch, and more.

Crimson Desert currently has a Metacritic score of 78. This is based on 98 scores ranging from several 100s to the lowest current score of 45. Insider Gaming scored the game a 7. You can check out our full review here.

Pearl Abyss’ stock price fell by nearly 30% after receiving some of the mediocre review scores. According to the Seoul Economic Daily, the market had anticipated a median score in the mid-to-high 80s, and the disappointing results seem to have negatively impacted the stock.

While it remains to be seen how the game performs, the metrics are at least high for now, and perhaps more buzz will be generated for the title, leading to positive user reviews in the future.

What do you think about the current response to Crimson Desert? Have you played the game so far? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
